headline: Tabung Haji's RM10 billion bailout still not fully explained
context: Seven years after the government injected RM10 billion to rescue Tabung Haji in 2018, a complete forensic audit has never been publicly released explaining how losses accumulated.

cards.big: RM10 billion Tabung Haji bailout — and still no public forensic audit after 7 years
cards.sub: 9 million depositors deserve to know how their pilgrimage savings were endangered

cards.big: Cabinet classified the full PwC forensic report under Official Secrets Act
cards.sub: Depositors can see their savings were at risk but cannot learn exactly why

cards.big: RM10 billion bailout equals RM1,111 per depositor across 9 million accounts
cards.sub: Public funds used to cover investment losses made without depositor knowledge

cards.big: Board members during loss period faced no disciplinary or legal consequences
cards.sub: Zero accountability for directors who oversaw billions in questionable investments

cards.big: Using OSA to hide a bailout report protects the people who caused the crisis, not national security
Should Tabung Haji depositors have a legal right to see the forensic audit report?
